items.title: Sam Merrill Undergoes MRI On Hamstring
items.blurb: Sam Merrill Undergoes MRI On Hamstring — Cleveland Cavaliers guard Sam Merrill underwent an MRI on his left hamstring on Wednesday. Merrill, who was held out of practice, suffered the injury during the Cavs' Game 1 loss to the Pistons. Merrill averaged 12.8 points in the regular season. Game 2 takes place on Thursday.

items.blurb: Notebook: Raptors reflect on season, series vs. Cavaliers — “It’s only the beginning.” Heading into the season, making the playoffs looked to be the goal. But after pushing the Cavs to the limit, the Raptors’ goal posts shifted. They’ll carry that new weight of expectation in next season, Kai Gammage writes.

items.title: Webster, Raptors’ off-season plan is simple: ‘More, better players’
items.blurb: Webster, Raptors’ off-season plan is simple: ‘More, better players’ — Toronto Raptors GM Bobby Webster discusses the team’s outlook heading into the offseason, saying that dipping into the luxury tax is always a possibility for a contending squad, but that the Raptors are still looking to bolster their overall talent.

items.title: Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic already looking ahead to challenge of next season
items.blurb: Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic already looking ahead to challenge of next season — If 46 wins and a playoff spot is the benchmark, the room for error for the Raptors is shrinking. Coach Darko Rajakovic said he’s up for the challenge, writes Michael Grange.

items.title: Raptors face several challenges in bid to maintain momentum in off-season
items.blurb: Raptors face several challenges in bid to maintain momentum in off-season — It was an enjoyable, refreshing season, but keeping the momentum rolling will be a tough needle to thread for the Raptors. Michael Grange looks at the situation for GM Bobby Webster.
